title: The Shadow in the Mirror
summary: Shodo meets Vex, a lonely echo-sprite, at the Bubble Lake and uses empathy to reach him.
purpose: Empathy can help us understand someone whose loneliness has led them to make harmful choices.
body: Shodo stepped into the Fairy Kingdom, but something felt wrong. The sky was a deep, dark purple, and Queen Bubble looked very worried.
Shodo ran to the water. Instead of her own reflection, she saw her bedroom back home, but everything in it was floating in the air! Then, the picture changed to a scary, cold, metal fortress.
Suddenly, a voice echoed out of the water. It didn't sound like a fairy. It sounded like a thousand tiny mirrors clinking together.

Out of the black water, a shadow stretched upward. It looked like a boy, but his skin shimmered like a broken mirror, and his eyes were dark and hollow. He drifted toward Shodo, his feet never touching the ground. He held a stolen, glowing memory, and it was fading in his grip.

“You're a long way from home, Bridge-Girl,” Vex said, his voice cold and sharp. He leaned closer, and Shodo felt a chill go through her, like a winter wind in the middle of summer. “I’m tired of the silence. If I can't have a home, then nobody gets to keep their memories.”

He reached out a cold, sharp hand toward Shodo’s face. The air around him felt heavy and sad. Shodo’s heart raced, but she looked straight into his dark, lonely eyes. She didn't run. She didn't fight. Instead, she took a deep breath.
“You’re not a monster, Vex,” Shodo said, her voice clear and brave. “You’re just lonely.”
Vex froze. His hand stopped in mid-air. For the first time, the dark purple light in his eyes flickered, and he looked… surprised.
